name: Bodb Dearg
description: Bodb Dearg, a prominent figure in Irish mythology, is a son of the Dagda, one of the most important gods in the Tuatha Dé Danann. He is best known for succeeding his father as High King of the Tuatha Dé Danann after their defeat by the Milesians. Despite not being the first choice for the kingship, Bodb Dearg's wisdom, fairness, and political acumen allowed him to maintain peace and prosperity amongst his people for many years. He is often associated with magic, particularly shapeshifting, and is said to have possessed great druidic powers. His name, meaning 'Red Crow' or 'Red Raven', may allude to his connection with prophecy and the Otherworld. Bodb Dearg resided at Síd ar Femin, one of the most significant sídhe mounds in Ireland, where he held court and dispensed justice. He played a crucial role in many of the tales surrounding the Tuatha Dé Danann, often acting as a mediator and counselor. His reign was marked by both internal strife and external threats, but he consistently demonstrated his ability to navigate these challenges and maintain the unity of his people. He is remembered as a wise and just ruler who prioritized the well-being of the Tuatha Dé Danann above all else. Bodb Dearg's influence extends beyond his reign, as he continues to be a symbol of leadership, wisdom, and magical prowess in Irish folklore. He is a powerful figure representing the enduring strength and resilience of the Tuatha Dé Danann. His story is a testament to the importance of diplomacy, strategic thinking, and unwavering commitment to one's people.
subtitle: High King of the Tuatha Dé Danann

keyMyths.title: Succession to the Kingship
keyMyths.description: After the Dagda's death, there was disagreement about who should be king. Despite not being the initial choice, Bodb Dearg's wisdom and fairness eventually led to his election.

keyMyths.title: The Children of Lir
keyMyths.description: Bodb Dearg played a role in the tragic tale of the Children of Lir, ensuring Aoife was punished for her cruelty and attempting to ease Lir's suffering.
keyMyths.source: The Fate of the Children of Lir

keyMyths.title: Residence at Síd ar Femin
keyMyths.description: Bodb Dearg's court was located at Síd ar Femin, a place of great power and importance. It was from here that he ruled and dispensed justice to the Tuatha Dé Danann.

sources.author: Peter Berresford Ellis
sources.description: Comprehensive overview of Irish mythological figures.
sources.text: Comprehensive overview of Irish mythological figures.

sources.author: Lady Gregory
sources.description: Retelling of Irish myths and legends.
sources.text: Retelling of Irish myths and legends.
